title: Britain Courts Anthropic - TCR 04/05/26
description:
Britain is actively courting Anthropic for a major UK expansion including a potential dual stock listing, Anthropic's OpenClaw subscription ban shifted 135,000 autonomous agent instances to pay-as-you-go billing, hackers weaponized Anthropic's leaked Claude Code within days, and researchers built a quantum battery that charges faster as it grows larger.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-5-2026/

The British government is formally pursuing Anthropic for a major UK expansion and dual stock listing, treating the company's safety commitments as a sovereign industrial asset after its confrontation with the Pentagon drove commercial adoption to record levels. Anthropic's decision to block OpenClaw from flat-rate subscriptions exposed the first structural collision between subscription pricing and autonomous AI agents - 135,000 instances generating compute demand that the $200-per-month model was never designed to absorb. Hackers embedded infostealer malware into reposted copies of Anthropic's leaked Claude Code source, weaponizing the 50,000-plus forks through poisoned repositories and fake installation guides promoted via sponsored search ads. A Zhejiang University study found that training AI vision models on human brain signals improved abstract reasoning by 20.5%, outperforming models with far more parameters and challenging the assumption that scaling alone drives intelligence. RMIT and CSIRO researchers built the first working quantum battery prototype - a device that charges faster as it grows larger, inverting conventional energy storage constraints. And Australian EV dealership lots emptied within days as the Iran conflict pushed diesel past AU$3 per litre, with BYD selling up to 800 vehicles per day in Queensland.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-april-5-2026/
